segments.text: Welcome back. Today we're discussing the proposed education reform bill with our guest.
segments.label: Neutral

segments.text: Every child deserves a chance to succeed. When I visit schools and see kids without basic supplies, it breaks my heart.
segments.label: Appeal to Emotion

segments.text: If this bill fails, an entire generation of children will be left behind, unable to compete in the global economy.
segments.label: Fear Mongering

segments.text: Current standardized test scores show a 12-point gap between high and low income districts, a trend that has persisted for two decades.
segments.label: Neutral

segments.text: The education establishment's stranglehold on our children's futures must be broken by bold, patriotic reform.
segments.label: Loaded Language

segments.text: You're either investing in America's future, or you're voting to keep the corrupt status quo. Pick a side.
segments.label: False Dichotomy

segments.text: Dr. Huang, who chaired the National Education Commission, reviewed our proposal and gave it her full endorsement.
segments.label: Appeal to Authority

segments.text: Parents across the country are rallying behind this bill. Thousands show up to town halls demanding change. You can't ignore that momentum.
segments.label: Bandwagon

segments.text: Teacher training programs vary widely in quality. Our proposal sets a national baseline while preserving state flexibility.
segments.label: Neutral

segments.text: Think of the teachers spending their own money on classroom supplies, working nights and weekends because they love these kids.
segments.label: Appeal to Emotion

segments.text: Without federal oversight, predatory charter operators will swoop in and siphon public funds while delivering nothing for students.
segments.label: Fear Mongering

segments.text: The radical voucher scheme is nothing more than a Trojan horse to dismantle the public education system brick by brick.
segments.label: Loaded Language

segments.text: Budget allocation data shows per-pupil spending ranges from $8,000 to $24,000 across districts — a significant funding disparity.
segments.label: Neutral

segments.text: The opposing side has no real solutions. Their own legislative record shows zero meaningful education bills passed in ten years.
segments.label: Ad Hominem

segments.text: Educators, parents, economists — experts from every field agree this reform is long overdue. The evidence is overwhelming.
segments.label: Appeal to Authority

segments.text: Join the millions demanding better schools. Be part of the solution, not part of the problem.
segments.label: Bandwagon
